title: 三只瞎老鼠
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\151-三只瞎老鼠-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Mr. Satterthwaite expresses concern to Mr. Quinn that visiting his old home might be disappointing due to changes over time. Mr. Satterthwaite insists that Mr. Quinn accompany him to visit Tom Addison, whom he describes as a hospitable man. During their conversation in a shop, a woman named Berilla Gillingham enters the china department to purchase colorful tea cups because some broke that morning. Mr. Satterthwaite recognizes her as the second wife of Simon Gillett and the wife of Tom Addison. 

Mrs. Gillingham identifies Mr. Satterthwaite as the guest expected for tea that afternoon at Doverton Grange. Mr. Satterthwaite apologizes for his delay, citing a car breakdown. He offers Mrs. Gillingham a ride to Doverton Kingsburn, but she declines, stating she must ride her motorcycle to pick up her children. Mr. Satterthwaite introduces Mr. Quinn to Mrs. Gillingham and asks if Tom Addison would welcome an extra guest, to which she agrees. However, Mr. Quinn declines the invitation, citing a prior appointment.

As Mr. Quinn prepares to depart, Mr. Satterthwaite asks for an explanation regarding their meeting. Mr. Quinn provides no direct answer but tells Mr. Satterthwaite that the word "colorblind" might be useful to him. After Mr. Quinn leaves, Mr. Satterthwaite departs in his repaired car toward Doverton Grange, repeatedly pondering the meaning of the word "colorblind." He reflects on his childhood memories of the estate's lawns, lake, and river. The passage concludes with a description of the tea setting arranged on the lawn, featuring ornate tables, garden chairs with colorful cushions, and a backdrop of a copper beech and a cedar of Lebanon tree.
